header.title: RNN vs Transformer
header.subtitle: Explore tokenization and normalized projections of hidden states and attention — structural trends, not semantic ground truth.

disclaimer.title: About these visualizations
disclaimer.body: Attention weights and hidden states are shown as normalized visualization projections to explore structural trends. They do not represent absolute semantic strength, importance, or individual neuron meaning.

input.title: Enter text
input.description: Type a prefix (e.g. “The apple is”) — both tiny models trained on WikiText-2 predict the next token while the panels below show internal dynamics.
input.placeholder: Try: The cat sat on the mat…

token.title: Tokenization
token.description: Watch BERT WordPiece split your sentence into subword tokens.

flow.title: Information flow (schematic)
flow.description: Schematic view of how each architecture routes information — illustrative, not a literal neuron-level map.
flow.meshOmitted: Cross-links omitted for long sequences — see attention heatmap below.
flow.rnnCaption: One token at a time: each step merges into a new hidden state hₜ (trained tiny LSTM). Watch the chain grow left → right.
flow.transformerCaption: All tokens at once: curved arcs are pairwise attention paths in parallel (causal self-attention, last layer).

prediction.title: Next-token prediction
prediction.description: Both models were trained on WikiText-2 for next-token prediction. Lower validation perplexity = better language modeling.
prediction.note: Predictions use the BERT tokenizer vocabulary. Try “The apple is” — models may suggest continuations like “,”, “a”, or “not” depending on training.

rnn.title: RNN (LSTM)
rnn.description: Tiny LSTM (64→128, WikiText-2): hidden-state vectors per step. The bar and color grid are normalized projections of vector magnitude / components — not semantic scores.
rnn.vectorTooltip: Component {value} — normalized color only, not neuron activation semantics

transformer.title: Transformer
transformer.description: Tiny causal Transformer (2 layers, 4 heads). Self-attention from the last layer, normalized for display. Cell brightness shows attention routing strength from query token (Y-axis) to key token (X-axis).
transformer.cellTooltip: Normalized attention {from} → {to}: {value} (relative weight in this projection)

metrics.note: Architecture-specific structure probes (not directly comparable side-by-side). For model quality, use val perplexity in the prediction panel above.

rnn.localHint: Average magnitude of hidden-state updates between consecutive tokens.
rnn.spreadHint: Cosine similarity between consecutive hidden states — sequential smoothness.
rnn.longRangeHint: How similar late hidden states are to the first step — drops when early context is washed out along the chain.

transformer.localHint: Share of attention mass on neighboring token pairs.
transformer.spreadHint: How peaked each row is (1 − normalized entropy) — sharp vs diffuse routing.
transformer.longRangeHint: Average token distance each query attends to — direct access lets later tokens reach farther back in one step.

errors.emptyText: Please enter some text to analyze.
errors.backendFailed: Failed to reach the backend. Make sure the FastAPI server is running on port 8000.
